code: @Manual{,   title = {spaghetti: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator},   author = {Jan Marvin Garbuszus},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.0}, }

title: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator
text: Bidirectional translator Excel user-facing formulas (seen formula bar) OOXML storage format (found inside .xlsx XML). Key features: - Automatic prefix injection (_xlfn., _xlws., _xlpm.) Excel 365+ functions - Spill operator (#) implicit intersection (@) translation - LAMBDA LET parameter scoping _xlpm. prefixes - Localised formula support (translate languages) - Vectorized — works single formulas character vectors - Formula linting spelling suggestions typos

title: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator
text: write formula openxlsx2::wb_add_formula(), string goes directly XML. Excel 365 functions like LAMBDA, XLOOKUP, FILTER, SEQUENCE need namespace prefixes storage never type formula bar: Without prefixes formula opens #NAME? Excel. spaghetti handles translation don’t remember rules.

title: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator
text: Via remotes: Via r-universe:

title: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator
code: library(spaghetti)  # Single formula to_xml("=SEQUENCE(10)") #> [1] "=_xlfn.SEQUENCE(10)"  # Vector of formulas - same function! to_xml(c(   "=SEQUENCE(10)",   "=FILTER(A1:A10, B1:B10 > 5)",   "=SUM(A1#)" )) #> [1] "=_xlfn.SEQUENCE(10)"                     #> [2] "=_xlfn._xlws.FILTER(A1:A10, B1:B10 > 5)" #> [3] "=SUM(_xlfn.ANCHORARRAY(A1))"  # Round-trip with localisation from_xml("=_xlfn.SEQUENCE(10)", locale = "de") #> [1] "=SEQUENZ(10)"

what: Example 1 — Dynamic array formulas (SEQUENCE, UNIQUE, XLOOKUP)
title: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator
text: common pain point: Excel 365 functions return spilled arrays. Write plain formula-bar syntax; to_xml() adds required prefixes handing openxlsx2. Works single formulas vectors.
code: library(spaghetti) library(openxlsx2)  # Some data to work with products <- data.frame(   id    = c(3, 1, 2, 1, 3, 2),   name  = c("Apple", "Banana", "Cherry", "Banana", "Apple", "Cherry"),   sales = c(120, 85, 200, 95, 110, 175) )  wb <- wb_workbook() |>   wb_add_worksheet("Data") |>   wb_add_data(x = products, dims = "A1")  # UNIQUE + SORT: deduplicated sorted product list spilling from E2 wb <- wb |>   wb_add_data(     dims = "E1", x = "Unique Products"   ) |>   wb_add_formula(     dims = "E2",     x = to_xml("=UNIQUE(SORT(B2:B7))"),     cm = TRUE   )  # XLOOKUP: look up total sales for each unique product using the spill ref # to_xml() works with single formulas or vectors wb <- wb |>   wb_add_data(     dims = "F1", x = "Total Sales"   ) |>   wb_add_formula(     dims = "F2",     x = to_xml("=XLOOKUP(E2#, B2:B7, C2:C7, 0, 0)"),     cm = TRUE   )  if (interactive()) wb$open()

what: Example 2 — LAMBDA and LET for reusable logic
title: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator
text: LAMBDA parameters need additional _xlpm. prefix inside XML — something ’s nearly impossible remember manually.
code: library(spaghetti) library(openxlsx2)  # Temperature conversion table temps_f <- data.frame(city = c("Berlin", "Paris", "Oslo"),                       temp_f = c(35.6, 42.8, 28.4))  wb <- wb_workbook() |>   wb_add_worksheet("Weather") |>   wb_add_data(x = temps_f, dims = "A1")  # Column header wb <- wb_add_data(wb, x = "Temp °C", dims = "C1")  # LAMBDA applied inline to each row with to_xml() # to_xml() handles character vectors directly formulas_lambda <- to_xml(   sprintf(     "=LAMBDA(f, (f - 32) * 5/9)(B%s)",     2:4   ) )  wb <- wb_add_formula(wb, dims = "C2", x = formulas_lambda, cm = TRUE)   # LET: named intermediate variables for a more complex calculation # Wet-bulb temperature approximation — readable formula using LET let <- paste0(     "=LET(",     "tc, (B%s-32)*5/9, ",        # Celsius     "rh, 0.6, ",                 # assume 60% relative humidity     "tc * ATAN(0.151977 * (rh * 100 + 8.313659) ^ 0.5)",     ")" ) formulas_let <- to_xml(   sprintf(let, 2:4) )  wb <- wb_add_data(wb, x = "Wet Bulb °C", dims = "D1") |>   wb_add_formula(     dims = "D2",     x = formulas_let,     cm = TRUE   )  if (interactive()) wb$open()

what: Example 3 — Reading back OOXML formulas and translating for display
title: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator
text: load existing .xlsx wb_to_df(show_formula = TRUE) inspect wb$worksheets, formulas come back OOXML storage format prefixes intact. from_xml() strips back readable form — useful auditing, diffing, displaying formulas users.
code: library(spaghetti) library(openxlsx2)  # Simulate loading an .xlsx that was originally saved by Excel # (it will have all the _xlfn. prefixes already present) wb <- wb_workbook() |>   wb_add_worksheet("Report") |>   wb_add_data(x = data.frame(x = 1:10, y = rnorm(10)), dims = "A1") |>   wb_add_formula(dims = "C2", x = to_xml("=SEQUENCE(5)"), cm = TRUE) |>   wb_add_formula(dims = "D2", x = to_xml("=XLOOKUP(C2#, A2:A11, B2:B11)"), cm = TRUE)  # Read back the raw formula strings as stored in the XML raw_formulas <- wb_to_df(wb, dims = "C2:D2", show_formula = TRUE, col_names = FALSE)  # The formulas come back with prefixes — not user-friendly # C2: =_xlfn.SEQUENCE(5) # D2: =_xlfn.XLOOKUP(_xlfn.ANCHORARRAY(C2), A2:A11, B2:B11)  # Translate the whole data frame of formula strings back to readable form readable <- as.data.frame(lapply(raw_formulas, function(col) {   ifelse(is_ooxml(col), from_xml(col), col) }))  # C2: =SEQUENCE(5) # D2: =XLOOKUP(C2#, A2:A11, B2:B11) print(readable) #>              C                             D #> 1 =SEQUENCE(5) =XLOOKUP(C2#, A2:A11, B2:B11)  # Bonus: translate to German for a localised formula audit report readable_de <- as.data.frame(lapply(raw_formulas, function(col) {   ifelse(is_ooxml(col), from_xml(col, locale = "de"), col) })) # D2: =XVERWEIS(C2#, A2:A11, B2:B11) print(readable_de) #>             C                              D #> 1 =SEQUENZ(5) =XVERWEIS(C2#; A2:A11; B2:B11)

title: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator
text: openxlsx2 accepts English function names. formulas authored localised application (e.g. German SUMMEWENN instead SUMIF), pass locale argument to_xml() translate first. Setup required: run setup_terminology() per machine download function-name translation data. Without , locale calls error. Supported locales: de, fr, es, , nl, pt, pl, sv, others …
code: # German formula → OOXML (translates SUMMEWENNS, SVERWEIS, etc.) to_xml("=SUMMEWENNS(C2:C10; A2:A10; \"Berlin\")", locale = "de") #> [1] "=SUMIFS(C2:C10, A2:A10, \"Berlin\")"  round_trip("=SUMMEWENNS(C2:C10; A2:A10; \"Berlin\")", locale = "de", out_locale = "es") #> $xml #> [1] "=SUMIFS(C2:C10, A2:A10, \"Berlin\")" #>  #> $formula #> [1] "=SUMAR.SI.CONJUNTO(C2:C10; A2:A10; \"Berlin\")"

title: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator
text: check_formula() validates function names suggests corrections typos:
code: # Catch typos before they become #NAME? errors check_formula("=SUIM(A1:A10)") #>         formula   fn     suggestion #> 1 =SUIM(A1:A10) SUIM SUM, DSUM, SIN  check_formula(c(   "=VLOKUP(A1, B:C, 2, 0)",   "=FLITER(A1:A10, B1:B10 > 0)" )) #>                       formula     fn               suggestion #> 1      =VLOKUP(A1, B:C, 2, 0) VLOKUP VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, LOOKUP #> 2 =FLITER(A1:A10, B1:B10 > 0) FLITER    FILTER, FISHER, FIXED  # Works with localised formulas too check_formula("=SUMMEWENNS(C2:C10; A2:A10; \"Berlin\")", locale = "de") #> No unknown functions found.

title: Check a formula for unknown function names — check_formula
Check a formula for unknown function names — check_formula
text: formula Character scalar vector formulas. locale Locale code NULL. set, localised names translated checking.

title: Check a formula for unknown function names — check_formula
text: data frame columns formula, fn, suggestion. fn unknown function name. suggestion comma-separated string close matches, NA suggestion found. Returns empty data frame (invisibly) issues found.

title: Check a formula for unknown function names — check_formula
text: Unlike to_xml(), translate — pure linting pass.

title: Check a formula for unknown function names — check_formula
code: check_formula("=SUIM(A1:A10)")          # typo: SUIM -> SUM #>         formula   fn     suggestion #> 1 =SUIM(A1:A10) SUIM SUM, DSUM, SIN check_formula("=VLOKUP(A1,B:B,2,0)")    # typo: VLOKUP -> VLOOKUP #>               formula     fn               suggestion #> 1 =VLOKUP(A1,B:B,2,0) VLOKUP VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, LOOKUP check_formula(c("=SUM(A1)", "=FLITER(A1:A10,B1:B10>0)")) #>                    formula     fn            suggestion #> 1 =FLITER(A1:A10,B1:B10>0) FLITER FILTER, FISHER, FIXED

what: Download and parse the Microsoft Terminology Collection. — setup_terminology
title: Download and parse the Microsoft Terminology Collection. — setup_terminology
text: Source()s parser script bundled inst/extdata/parse_locales.R, calls download_and_parse_tbx() function fetch zip Microsoft's public download URL, validate SHA-256 (supply one), unzip, parse, write resulting translation table per-user cache directory.

title: Download and parse the Microsoft Terminology Collection. — setup_terminology
code: setup_terminology(   expected_sha256 = .MTC_EXPECTED_SHA256,   force = FALSE,   workers = max(1L, parallel::detectCores() - 1L),   quiet = FALSE )

title: Download and parse the Microsoft Terminology Collection. — setup_terminology
text: expected_sha256 SHA-256 hex digest expected downloaded zip. Defaults digest version zip known release spaghetti. mismatch produces warning (error), since Microsoft may republish file. Pass NULL skip check entirely. force TRUE, re-download even cache exists. workers Number parallel TBX-parsing workers. Default detects cores - 1, capped 8. quiet TRUE, suppress progress messages.

title: Download and parse the Microsoft Terminology Collection. — setup_terminology
text: Invisibly, path cached RDS.

title: Download and parse the Microsoft Terminology Collection. — setup_terminology

what: spaghetti: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator — spaghetti-package
title: spaghetti: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator — spaghetti-package
text: Translates spreadsheet formulas user-facing format (displayed end user) OOXML storage format (found .xlsx XML source).

title: spaghetti: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator — spaghetti-package
text: to_xml(): user-facing form → OOXML storage from_xml(): OOXML storage → user-facing form function_prefix(): inspect prefix tier function name supported_locales(): list available locale codes round_trip(): convert back-convert testing

title: spaghetti: Bidirectional Spreadsheet-Formula to OOXML Translator — spaghetti-package
text: Non-English locales ship translated function names. Pass locale = "de" (German), "fr" (French), "es" (Spanish), etc. to_xml() translate local names English prefixing, from_xml() output localised names. Supported locales: af, sq, -ET, ar, hy, -, az-Latn, bn, eu, , bs-Cyrl, bs-Latn, bg, , ca, ckb, chr-Cher, zh-Hans, zh-Hant, hr, cs, da, prs-AF, nl, et, fil-PH, fi, fr, ff, gl, ka, de, el, gu, ha-Latn-NG, , hi, hu, , ig-NG, id, iu-Latn, ga-IE, xh-ZA, zu-ZA, , ja, qut-GT, kn, kk, km-KH, rw-RW, sw, kok, ko, ky, lo, lv, lt, lb-LU, mk, ms, ml-, mt-MT, mi-NZ, mr, mn, ne-NP, nb-, nn-, -, ps-AF, fa, pl, pt-BR, pt-PT, pa-Arab, pa-Guru, quz, ro, ru, gd, sr-Cyrl, sr-Latn, nso-ZA, tn-ZA, sd, si-LK, sk, sl, es, sv, tg-Cyrl-TJ, ta, tt-Cyrl, te, th, ti, tr, tk-TM, uk, ur, ug, uz-Cyrl, uz-Latn, ca-ES-valencia, vi, guc, cy-GB, wo-SN, yo-NG.

title: Convert a user-facing formula to OOXML storage format — to_xml
text: Convert user-facing formula OOXML storage format

title: Convert a user-facing formula to OOXML storage format — to_xml
Convert a user-facing formula to OOXML storage format — to_xml
text: formula Character scalar vector. Formula(s), without =. locale Two-letter locale code ("de", "fr", …) NULL. set, localised function names translated English locale argument separator (; many European locales) accepted. warn_unknown Logical; warn unknown function names (default TRUE).

title: Convert a user-facing formula to OOXML storage format — to_xml
text: Character scalar vector: OOXML formula(s) starting =.

title: Convert a user-facing formula to OOXML storage format — to_xml
code: to_xml("=SEQUENCE(10)") #> [1] "=_xlfn.SEQUENCE(10)" to_xml("=LAMBDA(temp, (5/9) * (temp-32))(100)") #> [1] "=_xlfn.LAMBDA(_xlpm.temp, (5/9) * (_xlpm.temp-32))(100)" to_xml("=FILTER(A1:A10, B1:B10 > 5)") #> [1] "=_xlfn._xlws.FILTER(A1:A10, B1:B10 > 5)" to_xml("=SUM(A1#)") #> [1] "=SUM(_xlfn.ANCHORARRAY(A1))" to_xml("=LET(tc,(B2-32)*5/9,rh,0.6,tc*ATAN(0.151977*(rh*100+8.313659)^0.5))") #> [1] "=_xlfn.LET(_xlpm.tc,(B2-32)*5/9,_xlpm.rh,0.6,_xlpm.tc*ATAN(0.151977*(_xlpm.rh*100+8.313659)^0.5))" if (FALSE) to_xml("=SUMMEWENN(A1:A10;\"x\";B1:B10)", locale = "de") # \dontrun{} to_xml(c("=SUM(A1:A10)", "=SEQUENCE(5)", "=FILTER(A1:A10, B1:B10 > 0)")) #> [1] "=SUM(A1:A10)"                            #> [2] "=_xlfn.SEQUENCE(5)"                      #> [3] "=_xlfn._xlws.FILTER(A1:A10, B1:B10 > 0)"

what: Tokenise and display a formula as an ASCII tree — xlex
title: Tokenise and display a formula as an ASCII tree — xlex
text: Parses formula using spaghetti's lexer renders token tree style tidyxl::xlex(). Nesting follows call structure: tokens inside function's parentheses appear children function node.

title: Tokenise and display a formula as an ASCII tree — xlex
Tokenise and display a formula as an ASCII tree — xlex
text: formula Character scalar. Formula without leading =. locale Two-letter locale code NULL. Used select correct argument separator (; German etc.) translate localised function names display label. print Logical. Print tree console (default TRUE). Set FALSE get data frame silently.

title: Tokenise and display a formula as an ASCII tree — xlex
text: data frame columns depth, val, label, invisibly. depth nesting level (0 = root, 1 = top-level arguments, …). Printed side-effect print = TRUE.

title: Tokenise and display a formula as an ASCII tree — xlex
text: formula displayed -(OOXML translation). Pass result to_xml() want inspect storage form.

title: Tokenise and display a formula as an ASCII tree — xlex
code: xlex("=SUM(A1:A10)") #> root  #> °-- SUM            function  #>    °-- (           fun_open  #>       ¦-- A1:A10   ref  #>       °-- )        fun_close  xlex("=IF(A1>0, VLOOKUP(A1, B:C, 2, 0), NA())") #> root  #> °-- IF                function  #>    °-- (              fun_open  #>       ¦-- A1          ref  #>       ¦-- >           operator  #>       ¦-- 0           number  #>       ¦-- ,           separator  #>       ¦--             operator  #>       ¦-- VLOOKUP     function  #>       ¦   °-- (       fun_open  #>       ¦      ¦-- A1   ref  #>       ¦      ¦-- ,    separator  #>       ¦      ¦--      operator  #>       ¦      ¦-- B    ref  #>       ¦      ¦-- :    operator  #>       ¦      ¦-- C    ref  #>       ¦      ¦-- ,    separator  #>       ¦      ¦--      operator  #>       ¦      ¦-- 2    number  #>       ¦      ¦-- ,    separator  #>       ¦      ¦--      operator  #>       ¦      ¦-- 0    number  #>       ¦      °-- )    fun_close  #>       ¦-- ,           separator  #>       ¦--             operator  #>       ¦-- NA          function  #>       ¦   °-- (       fun_open  #>       ¦      °-- )    fun_close  #>       °-- )           fun_close  xlex("=LAMBDA(x, x * 2)(5)") #> root  #> ¦-- LAMBDA     function  #> ¦   °-- (      fun_open  #> ¦      ¦-- x   ref  #> ¦      ¦-- ,   separator  #> ¦      ¦--     operator  #> ¦      ¦-- x   ref  #> ¦      ¦--     operator  #> ¦      ¦-- *   operator  #> ¦      ¦--     operator  #> ¦      ¦-- 2   number  #> ¦      °-- )   fun_close  #> ¦-- (          fun_open  #> ¦-- 5          number  #> °-- )          fun_close  xlex("=SUMMEWENNS(C2:C10; A2:A10; \"Berlin\")", locale = "de") #> root  #> °-- SUMMEWENNS       function  #>    °-- (             fun_open  #>       ¦-- C2:C10     ref  #>       ¦-- ;          separator  #>       ¦--            operator  #>       ¦-- A2:A10     ref  #>       ¦-- ;          separator  #>       ¦--            operator  #>       ¦-- "Berlin"   text  #>       °-- )          fun_close  # Inspect OOXML form xlex(to_xml("=FILTER(A1:A10, B1:B10 > 0)")) #> root  #> °-- _xlfn._xlws.FILTER   function  #>    °-- (                 fun_open  #>       ¦-- A1:A10         ref  #>       ¦-- ,              separator  #>       ¦--                operator  #>       ¦-- B1:B10         ref  #>       ¦--                operator  #>       ¦-- >              operator  #>       ¦--                operator  #>       ¦-- 0              number  #>       °-- )              fun_close

title: spaghetti 0.3.0
text: Locale translation data longer bundled package. Previously, inst/extdata/excel_functions.rds shipped pre-built function-name translation table extracted Microsoft Terminology Collection. Microsoft publish explicit license Terminology Collection contents, package now ships parser. Users invoke setup_terminology() per machine download (~100 MB) parse data per-user cache directory (tools::R_user_dir("spaghetti", "data")). to_xml() / from_xml() / check_formula() calls non-NULL locale argument now error terminology cache built. error message directs user setup_terminology(). Calls locale = NULL (default) work setup required. R version requirement bumped R >= 4.0.0 (tools::R_user_dir).

title: spaghetti 0.3.0
text: setup_terminology(expected_sha256, force, workers, quiet): downloads Microsoft Terminology Collection zip Microsoft’s public download URL, verifies known-good SHA-256 digest (overridable; mismatches warn abort, since Microsoft can republish file time), unzips, parses, writes cached RDS. Pass expected_sha256 = NULL skip verification. Thin R wrapper around parser script inst/extdata/parse_locales.R. terminology_info(): returns provenance attributes attached cached RDS download time — source URL, observed SHA-256, timestamp, spaghetti version, cache path, function count, locale count. Useful verifying version Microsoft data currently loaded. has_terminology(): returns TRUE terminology cache loaded. clear_terminology(): removes cached RDS. inst/extdata/parse_locales.R gains download_and_parse_tbx() function R wrapper invokes. script can also sourced manually (source(system.file("extdata", "parse_locales.R", package = "spaghetti"))) users want drive parser .

title: spaghetti 0.3.0
text: openxlsx2 digest declared Suggests; setup_terminology() checks runtime. .onLoad() looks cached RDS user data directory; found loads silently, otherwise leaves locale tables empty.

title: spaghetti 0.2.0
text: round_trip() now returns list elements xml formula. previous element name excel (earlier locale, bug). Update code reading rt$excel rt$locale rt$formula. LEGACY function registry split LEGACY_WORKSHEET (modern worksheet functions) LEGACY_XLM (Excel 4 macro language). External code accessing spaghetti:::.spaghetti_env$LEGACY must use one new names. function_prefix() continues return "legacy" . R/Zzz.R file renamed R/zzz.R match standard convention fix incorrect documentation ASCII source order. IDENT tokens (LAMBDA / LET parameters, named ranges) longer passed locale lookup from_xml(). previous behaviour mis-translate LAMBDA parameter named e.g. sum local function name SUM. relied locale-translation bare identifiers, breaking change.

title: spaghetti 0.2.0
text: Lexer now distinguishes dedicated REF token type IDENT. Cell references — including sheet-qualified, quoted-sheet, 3D, external-workbook, structured table refs — emitted single REF token: Sheet1!A1, 'Sheet'!A1 Sheet1:Sheet5!A1 (3D range across sheets) [Book1]Sheet1!A1, [1]Sheet1!A1, '[Book1.xlsx]Sheet1'!A1 Table1[Col], Table1[#Headers], Table1[@Col], Table1[[#],[Col]] @FUNC(...) now wraps _xlfn.SINGLE(FUNC(...)) instead dropping function call. Excel error literals (#REF!, #N/, #DIV/0!, #VALUE!, #NAME?, #NUM!, #NULL!, #GETTING_DATA, #SPILL!, #CALC!, #BLOCKED!, #FIELD!, #PYTHON!, etc.) lex opaque values rather fragmented # (anchor) + identifier. Array literals {...} lexed single opaque token. Internal column (,) row (;) separators longer normalised outer locale-separator pass, =SUM({1,2;3,4};A1) round-trips correctly German locale. User-typed whitespace inside ranges (A1 : B10) normalised compact form (A1:B10) to_xml() output. range-intersection operator (space two refs, A1:B10 C5:D15) preserved. Locale codes can now multi-segment (de-DE, pt-BR, zh-Hans, zh-Hans-CN). Lookup tries full string first falls back segment--segment (zh-Hans-CN → zh-Hans → zh). xlex() now labels three new token kinds explicitly: error error literals, array {...} literals, intersection space two refs.

title: spaghetti 0.2.0
text: LAMBDA / LET parameter detection longer prefixes named ranges appear body. rule : depth==1 innermost LAMBDA/LET, IDENT bound name iff next token ,. Number lexer longer consumes trailing +/- part number. 1+2 similar arithmetic now tokenises correctly. Scientific notation 1e-3, 1.5E+2 still works. is_ooxml() regex now detects _xlws. addition _xlfn. _xlpm.. .spell_suggest() orders results edit distance instead returning matches registry order. TEXTJOIN longer duplicated across LEGACY XLFN tiers. function_table() longer leaks internal term_id column. from_xml() recursively transforms inner tokens _xlfn.ANCHORARRAY(...) _xlfn.SINGLE(...), nested prefixed function calls inside wrapper get prefixes stripped names localised correctly. parse_locales.R longer auto-executes bottom--file call script source()d. Wrap (sys.nframe() == 0L && ...).

title: spaghetti 0.2.0
text: O(n²) list-growth eliminated .tokenise(), .transform_to_xml(), .transform_from_xml() preallocating output lists using amortised-growth indexing. Lexer switched per-character paste0(s, c) accumulation substring extraction, reducing character-level allocation long formulas. Locale lookups .locale_to_english() / .english_to_locale() now O(1) hashed environment lookups instead linear column scans per-call toupper(). .spell_suggest() check_formula() use cached ALL_KNOWN vector built .onLoad(). .get_sep() vapply hot loops now lifted outside loop body to_xml(), from_xml(), check_formula().

title: spaghetti 0.2.0
text: Shared semicolon-locales list moved hardcoded constant translate.R .spaghetti_env$SEMICOLON_LOCALES. Per-locale lookup environments (LOC_TO_EN, EN_TO_LOC) built .onLoad() function table. Imports: utils narrowed importFrom(utils, adist).

title: spaghetti 0.1.0
text: Initial release.
